From Ann Arbor.

Industry.

the economy's terrible, it's not a good time to get jobs.

… I don't know how much… did he fill you in, kind of, on this conversation, and….

… I mean, I am part of the marketing team, I'm, like, the director of creative and branding, so… But I… I'm on the marketing side, I do a lot of… I'm kind of like, I fill in a lot of the marketing stuff that has holes.

Okay, that's… that's fair.
Exactly.

Did you work with her directly?
I think that's how I probably have met you.
I've definitely… Yeah, I was probably in that meeting.
I was gonna say, you do look familiar, too.

kind of runs it… it's not a daycare, but it's like… it's like a WeWork with a daycare type of thing, so you can share collaborative workspace, but bring your kids.
That's nice, for working moms.

No, Elise.

Absolutely, yeah.
So, and she sounds like she's doing really well, so… but, yeah, we miss her.

met with him last week, I'm like, Sky, you're very smart.

tech guy, as he called himself, right?

And so, he definitely found some interest in that, as well as our automated social tool.

As a way for you to kind of get more value, lesser price, you know, with the automated social in Google.

So we integrate with your property management software, we pull through your syndication, just like you would send it to, like, an ILS, and then we take all that information and run it through our large language models, which allows us to generate fresh, relevant, unique content for your Google and for your social pages.

This doesn't require you to go in and, like, approve anything or suggest any content, because we have that integration with your software.

pages.
